Level Builder

Bbox is auto-detected at runtime — just drop in any PNG with the object centred and transparent padding around it.
target cells
Ports the Unity LevelEditor pipeline (downsample + alpha-clip + colour-cluster) — converts any picture above into a tile cluster of roughly the requested cell count, with arrow directions assigned by the playable's own peel-off solver.
Pick a level .asset file from the Tap Gallery Unity project (Assets/Configs/Data/LevelData/*.asset). Width, height, every cell's position, arrow direction and colour are read directly from the file — no detection error margin.
Fallback for when the .asset file isn't available. Drop a clean game screenshot and the builder will guess tile size, grid, directions and 4 colour buckets via image analysis. Less reliable than Unity import.
Each toggle is baked into the exported playable's LEVEL.effects — disabled flags hide the corresponding visual via root classes (no-shadows, no-wrong-flash, etc.).
Letters track the hue: Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Cyan, Blue, Purple, Magenta, Light, Dark. Slots whose hue isn't present in the current level keep the playable's default fallback colour.
Upload either a JSON file with { colors, dirs, palette?, image? }, or a previously downloaded playable HTML — the builder will extract the LEVEL block and populate the form.
Click a level tab to edit its colors / dirs / image / palette. Effects, audio, monetization and background apply globally to all levels. The win endcard only fires after the final level.
Both grids must have identical empty/non-empty cells — every . in colors maps to . in dirs.
Loading template…
Solid colour (CSS, e.g. #ffd9a3)
Image (overrides colour, auto-resized to ≤1024px)
Leave blank to keep the playable's built-in WebAudio sounds. Files larger than 200 KB after compression trigger a status warning.
Preset
Custom upload (replaces preset)
PNG read as base64 and inlined; the playable streaks this texture along the slide direction. Length / opacity are in the Config section below.
Every field above is exported into LEVEL.config. Empty values fall back to the source-template defaults so the playable still works if you clear them.
In-game taps → auto-redirect (0 = only CTA redirects)
Android store URL
iOS store URL
CTA-click handler in the playable picks the right URL by user-agent (iOS / Android), tries mraid.open() first (Mintegral / AppLovin / Moloco / Unity), then window.clickTag (Google), and finally a plain window.open as a fallback.
_play _ _st_ xcl _ - {network}.{ext}
Example: . The Ncl token reflects the Monetization → In-game taps field (0 → xcl, 1 → 1cl, etc). The Pack button below always generates all three variants regardless.
